name: Thetis
description: Thetis is a Greek sea nymph, a Nereid, known for her shapeshifting abilities and her role as the mother of Achilles. She was the daughter of Nereus and Doris, making her one of the fifty Nereids who were nymphs of the sea. Zeus and Poseidon both pursued her, but they withdrew their advances after a prophecy revealed that Thetis would bear a son greater than his father. This led to her marriage to the mortal king Peleus. Despite her divine nature, she was bound by fate to bear a mortal son. She attempted to make Achilles immortal by dipping him in the River Styx, but the heel by which she held him remained vulnerable. Thetis played a significant role in the Trojan War, often intervening on Achilles' behalf, including securing him divine armor crafted by Hephaestus. She is a powerful figure associated with the sea, motherhood, and fate, and her story highlights the complex relationship between gods and mortals in Greek mythology. She possessed the power to shapeshift, prophecy, and divine influence over the sea.
subtitle: Sea nymph, mother of Achilles

keyMyths.title: Thetis and Peleus
keyMyths.description: Zeus and Poseidon gave up pursuing Thetis after a prophecy. She was then married to Peleus with the gods' blessing.

keyMyths.title: Achilles' Immortality
keyMyths.description: Thetis attempted to make Achilles immortal by dipping him in the River Styx. However, his heel remained vulnerable, leading to his eventual death.

keyMyths.title: Thetis and Hephaestus
keyMyths.description: Thetis obtained divine armor for Achilles from Hephaestus after Hector killed Patroclus. This armor played a crucial role in Achilles' revenge.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: A major source for the Trojan War and Thetis' role.
